violation.description: Non-load-bearing qualifier 'symbolic' inserted into the title ('the boundary of symbolic verification') and the opening claim ('when symbolic verification is cheap ... when symbolic verification is expensive'). The argument (oracle cost, discrimination, convergence evidence) never uses symbolic-ness; 'symbolic' appears nowhere else in the note.

Non-load-bearing qualifier 'typed' inserted into the title ('makes typed routing architectural') and the opening claim ('every typed routing mechanism that directs the agent ...'). The statelessness argument applies to all routing mechanisms listed (naming conventions, directory structure, triggers); nothing in the reasoning depends on typing.

Non-load-bearing qualifier 'deterministic' inserted into the title ('the boundary of deterministic verification') and the opening claim ('when deterministic verification is cheap ...'). The central argument turns on verification cost and per-instance discrimination, never on determinism; the word's only body occurrence is a parenthetical property list of hard oracles, and the oracle-strength spectrum the note builds on includes soft/non-deterministic oracles.

Non-load-bearing qualifier 'deterministic' inserted into the title ('makes deterministic routing architectural') and the opening claim ('every deterministic routing mechanism ...'). The argument covers prose routing surfaces (CLAUDE.md, skill descriptions) that are not deterministic at all; determinism is never used in the reasoning.
